original: John VII ends his reign as Catholic Pope
Church of the Holy Sepulchre in Jerusalem is destroyed by the Fatimid caliph Al-Hakim bi-Amr Allah, who hacks the church's foundations down to the bedrock
Danes led by Cnut defeat the Saxon forces of Edmund Ironside at the Battle of Assandun (Ashingdon), completing their conquest of England
Battle of Dyrrhachium: Southern Italian Normans under Robert Guiscard defeat Byzantine army under Alexios I Komnenos (modern Durrës, Albania)
Ukrainian city of Chernigov surrenders, despite this still sacked and pillaged by Mongolian army of Batu
Basel earthquake, the most significant historic seismological event north of the Alps, destroys Basel in Switzerland.
Heidelberg University is founded in modern-day Baden-Württemberg, Germany
New pursuit of French protestants
Fourth Battle of Kawanakajima: Takeda Shingen defeats Uesugi Kenshin in the climax of their ongoing conflict
John Hawkins begins 2nd trip to America
Spanish troops attack Maastricht
1st labor organization forms in North American colonies (Boston Shoemakers)
English fleet plunders Suriname plantations
Treaty of Buchach ends the first phase of the Polish–Ottoman War between the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth and the Ottoman Empire
Treaty of Aix-la-Chapelle signed, ending War of Austrian Succession
Premiere of Jean-Jacques Rousseau's opera "Le Devin du Village" (The Village Soothsayer), before the royal court at Fontainebleau, France
Boundary between Maryland and Pennsylvania, the Mason–Dixon line is agreed upon
Battle of Pelham: Colonel John Glover & Marblehead regiment meet British Forces in Bronx
In a bar decorated with bird tail in Elmsford, New York, a customer requests a glassful of “those cock tails” from bartender Betsy Flanagan
45 women meet at Mrs Silas Lee's home in Wiscasset, Maine, organizes Female Charitable Society, one of the earliest women's clubs in America
Second Opium War ends with the Convention of Peking and the ratification of the Treaty of Tientsin, an unequal treaty
Morgan's raiders capture federal garrison at Lexington, KY
Battle of Charlestown, WV
Boulaq Museum is inaugurated in Cairo with French Egyptologist Auguste Mariette as director - the basis of the Egyptian National Museum
1st football game between Toronto Argonauts & Hamilton Tigers
Columbia Princeton Rutgers & Yale set rules for collegiate football
Anti-socialist laws are ratified in Germany
Edison makes electricity available for household use
1st commercial long-distance phone line opens (Chicago-NY)
American flag raised in Puerto Rico
Count Bernard von Bulow becomes Chancellor of Germany, famous for first referring to Germany's need for 'place in the sun' and global empire
Belgium's Louise of den Plas begins activities towards women rights
Belgium annexes Congo Free State
Comte de Lambert of France sets airplane altitude record of 300 m
The First Balkan War breaks out, with Bulgaria, Serbia, and Greece opposed to Turkey
Schoenstatt Movement is founded in Germany
3rd Italian offensive at Isonzo
Czechoslovakia declares independence from Austro-Hungarian Empire
NHL's Quebec Bulldogs sold to a Toronto businessman P. J. Quinn
Russian 10th Army drives out White armies of Tsaritsyn (Stalingrad)
Belvin Maynard wins the first transcontinental air race in round trip of nine days, four hours, 25 minutes and 12 seconds, race costs nine lives with 54 crashes or forced landings
Biding its time, Soviet Russia agrees to independence for the Crimea
British Broadcasting Company (BBC) is founded, later called British Broadcasting Corporation
Notre Dame beats Army 13-7, NY Hearld Tribune dubs them (4 Horsemen)
-20] French Gen Sarrail bombs Damascus
Women are officially considered "persons" under Canadian law
Joseph Sylvester becomes 1st jockey to win 7 races in 1 day
Belgium government of Renkin falls
Kaufman & Harts "George Washington Slept Here" premieres in NYC
Soviet spy Richard Sorge arrested in Tokyo
US bombing of Bougainville, Solomon Island
Soviet troops invade Czechoslovakia during WW II
Operation 10 Plagues - Israeli offensive against Egyptian army
USSR performs nuclear test
Date of the first Mad Magazine issue
Vinoo Mankad takes 13 Pakistan wkts to win 1st India-Pak clash
Willie Thrower becomes 1st black NFL quarterback in modern times
WLJT TV channel 11 in Lexington, TN (PBS) begins broadcasting
WTVK TV channel 26 in Knoxville, TN (NBC) begins broadcasting
Hurricane Hazel becomes most severe to ever hit the US, killing 195 in US and Canada
Texas Instruments Inc. announces the first transistor radio
WBTW-TV channel 13 in Florence, South Carolina (CBS/ABC) begins broadcasting
WNBC radio in New York City changes call letters to WRCA
Buganda Agreement between Andrew Cohen, Governor of Uganda Protectorate, and Mutesa II, Kabaka of Buganda
In Britain, News Chronicle & Daily Mail merge and London Evening Star merges with Evening News
Emergency crisis proclaimed in South Vietnam due to communist attack
US launches Ranger 5 for lunar impact; misses moon
US performs atmospheric nuclear test at Johnston Atoll in the North Pacific Ocean
IOC votes Mexico City to host 1968 Olympics
Australian swimmer Kevin Berry sets world record 2:06.6 to beat American Carl Robie by 0.9s and win the men's 200m backstroke gold medal at the Tokyo Olympics

MLB American League votes to allow Kansas City Athletics to move to Oakland, California & expand league to 12 teams in 1971 with Kansas City & Seattle teams - later accelerated to 1969 due to pressure from Kansas City politicians
Nobel Prize in Physics awarded to German-American nuclear physicist Hans Bethe
Soviet Venera 4 becomes 1st probe to send data back from Venus
A rare Australian 1-2 in track & field; Maureen Caird in Olympic record 10.39s beats teammate Pam Kilborn by 0.07s to win the 80m hurdles at the Mexico City Games
American athletes sweep the medals in the men's 400m at the Mexico City Olympics; Lee Evans wins gold in world record 43.86s ahead of Larry James and Ron Freeman
American swimmer Don Schollander anchors US 4 × 200m freestyle relay team to world record 7:52.1 and the Olympic gold medal in Tokyo; Schollander's 4th gold of the Games
Circus Circus hotel opens in Las Vegas, largest permanent big top in the world
Polish sprinter Irena Szewińska runs a world record 22.58s to beat Australian Raelene Boyle by 0.16s and win the 200m gold medal at the Mexico City Olympics
Federal government bans use of cyclamates artificial sweeteners
Jefferson Airplane's Paul Kanter arrested for marijuana possession
Soyuz 8 returns to Earth
Sachio Kinugasa begins 2,215 cons game streak for Hiroshima Carp
Congress authorizes bi-centennial quarter, half-dollar & dollar coin
Nobel prize for Economic Sciences awarded to American Wassily Leontief
1st ABA regular season game at Market Square Arena in Indianapolis, Indiana - Pacers lose to San Antonio Spurs 129-121 in double overtime before 7,473 fans
Andre van de Louw  is appointed Mayor of Rotterdam, Netherlands
USSR performs nuclear test at Novaya Zemlya USSR
1st Islander 0-0 tie-Kings at Nassau-25th time shutout-Resch's 15th
NY Yankees win their 21st World Championship, 4 games
West German commandos liberate Boeing 737 with 86 hostages at Mogadishu
Yanks beat Dodgers 8-4 for 21st world championship, 1st in 15 years
NY Islanders 1st scoreless tie, vs LA Kings
US 1st daughter Susan Ford announces engagement to Charles F Vance
Stage musical "Beatlemania" opens in London
USSR performs nuclear test at Novaya Zemlya USSR
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Detroit blocks 21 Atlanta shots setting NBA record (double OT)
Andreas Papandreous' PASOK wins Greek elections
NY Giant Joe Danelo ties NFL record of 6 field goals in a game
Poland General Jaruzelski elected party leader
Discovery moves to Vehicle Assembly Building at Kennedy Space Center (Florida) for mating of STS 51A mission
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Israel's supreme court uphold's ban on Kahane`s Kach Party as racist
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Hungary revises its constitution after the fall of its Communist regime
US 62nd manned space mission STS 34 (Atlantis 5) launches into orbit
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
6.6 earthquake hits Colombia with no fatalities
Philadelphia Eagle Randall Cunningham sets NFL quarterback scramble record of 3,683
Start of Zimbabwe's 1st Test match, v India at Harare
STS-58 (Columbia) launches into orbit
NHL Winnipeg Jets sold to Americans who plan to move them to Phoenix
Crude Oil for November delivery falls to its lowest level since August 1999 on the New York Mercantile Exchange (NYMEX)
Bolivian Gas War: President Gonzalo Sánchez de Lozada, is forced to resign and leave Bolivia
British Mercedes driver Jenson Button finishes 5th in Brazilian Grand Prix at Autódromo José Carlos Pace to clinch his first F1 World Drivers Championship
Israeli soldier Gilad Shalit is released by Hamas
Google stock trading suspended after premature release of a quarterly report indicating a 20% drop in profits and a 9% fall in share price
Syrian military airstrikes kill 40 people in Maaret al-Numan
Saudi Arabia becomes the first country to turn down a seat on the UN Security Council in protest over Syria
US White Houses says it is "confident" Russia behind recent email hacking and attempts to influence US election
1st all-female spacewalk by NASA astronauts Christina Koch and Jessica Meir outside the International Space Station
Charges of disorderly conduct officially dropped against participants of the 1939 equal rights protest sit-in at Alexandria (Virginia) Library
Bolivia elects Luis Arce President and his party, Movement for Socialism back into government
Ecuador President Guillermo Lasso declares 60-day state of emergency in response to a violent crime wave caused by power struggle between drug cartels
Flash foods and heavy rain in northern Indian state of Uttarakhand and Nepal kill over 100 people after 328mm falls in 24 hrs
American cellist Yo-Yo Ma is awarded the fifth $1 million Birgit Nilsson Prize

Evidence found of lost star catalog by 2nd century BC Greek astronomer Hipparchus (earliest known attempt to record celestial objects' coordinates with naked eye) found in palimpsest manuscript published by scholars
At least 500 Palestinians killed in explosion on Al-Ahli Arab Hospital, Gaza Strip, with local officials blaming Israeli missile attack and Israelis blaming misfired Palestinian rocket
Power cut to Guinea-Bissau's capital Bissau by Turkish firm Karpowership over a unpaid $15 million bill
Cuba's power grid shuts down after a major power station fails, leaving 10 million people without power and closing schools and businesses
French KingLouis XIIIand leader of the Huguenots Duke Henry II of Rohan sign the Treaty of Montpellier ending hostilities
African-American poetPhillis Wheatleyfreed from slavery
Tadeusz Kościuszkoreceives a commission from US Congress as a colonel of engineers in the Continental Army
Friedrich Wilhelm Joseph Schellingis granted permission to enroll at the Tübinger Stift at an earlier age than usually permitted
Franz Liszt's symphonic poem "Prometheus" premieres
Henrik Ibsen's play "De Unges Forbund" (The League of Youth) premieres in Christiania (Oslo), Norway
Gustav Mahler's 5th symphony premieres in Cologne, Germany
E. M. Forsterpublishes his novel "Howards End"

Black boxerJack Johnsonarrested for violating the Mann Act for "transporting women across state lines for immoral purposes" due to his relationship with white woman Lucille Cameron, allegedly a prostitute. Later convicted by an all-white jury and sentenced to a year in prison.
David Beattyis created 1st Earl Beatty, Viscount Borodale, and Baron Beatty of the North Sea and Brooksby
Charles Stritegranted US patent #1,394,450 for his invention, the automatic pop-up toaster
Harold "Red" Grange, finest collegiate football game (4 long TD runs)
Salt Lake City (PCL)Tony Lazzerihits his 60th HR of the season
Frankfurter Zeitung publishesLenin's political testament
GangsterAl Caponeis convicted on five of the 23 counts of tax evasion against him, later fined $50,000 and sentenced to 11 years in jail
Hitlerorders captured allied commandos to be killed
Allied generalsDwight D. Eisenhower,Omar BradleyandBernard Montgomeryconfer in Brussels, Belgium
Paul Robesonwins Spingarn Medal for singing and acting achievements
Aaron Copland's 3rd Symphony first performance, with the Boston Symphony Orchestra led by Serge Koussevitzky
"Track & Field" magazine namesJesse Owensall-time track athlete
The antiproton (antiparticle of the proton) is discovered at University of California by physicists Emilio Segrè andOwen Chamberlain- wins 1959 Nobel Prize for Physics
"TheFrank SinatraShow" television variety/drama anthology series debuts on ABC; the singer-actor is given almost complete control of the program
Casey Stengelretired by NY Yankees (won 10 pennants in 12 years)
Film adaptation of the 1957 Broadway musical "West Side Story", directed by Robert Wise andJerome Robbins, and starringNatalie Wood, premieres (Academy Award - Best Picture, 1962)
NY's Museum of Modern Art displaysHenri Matisse's picture "Le Bateau" upside down, takes 47 days and 116,000 people before someone notices
James Watson(US),Francis Crick(UK) andMaurice Wilkins(UK) win the Nobel Prize for Medicine for their work in determining the structure of DNA
Tony Sheridan& Beat Brothers (also known as The Beatles) record "Let's Dance" in Hamburg, Germany
US PresidentJohn F. Kennedymeets Soviet Minister of Foreign Affairs Andrei Gromyko
"Persona", Swedish film directed byIngmar Bergman, starring Bibi Andersson and Liv Ullmann, is released
Walt Disney's animated musical adaptation ofRudyard Kipling's "The Jungle Book" film is released; cast features voices of Phil Harris, Louis Prima, Sterling Holloway, and Sebastian Cabot
Police find 219 grains of cannabis resin inJohn LennonandYoko Ono's apartment, they are fined £150 for marijuana possession
British singerRod Stewartjoins rock band Small Faces
American R&B singerAl Green's girlfriend, Mary Woodson, dies by suicide at his Memphis home, after assaulting him with a pot of boiling grits at 29
Chicago BullsNate Thurmond, in his debut with the team, becomes 1st in NBA player to complete a quadruple double: 22 points, 14 rebounds, 13 assists, and 12 blocks

The Country Hams release instrumental single "Walking in the Park with Eloise", written by James McCartney, and produced by his sonPaul McCartney: the group consisted of Wings  with guests Chet Atkins and Floyd Cramer
Simon & Garfunkel reunite on "Saturday Night Live", sing "My Little Town"
Nobel prize for chemistry awarded to AmericanWilliam Lipscomb
Iran'sAyatollah Khomeiniorders mass executions to stop
"Roseanne" TV comedy starringRoseanne Barr,John Goodmanand Laurie Metcalf premieres in the US on ABC
East German communist leaderErich Honeckerresigns
"My Own Private Idaho" directed and written by Gus van Sant starringRiver PhoenixandKeanu Reevesis released
Revival ofFrank Loesser's 1956 musical "The Most Happy Fella" closes at NY State Theater, NYC, after a limited run by the New York City Opera
The groundbreaking Guggenheim Museum, designed byFrank Gehry, is inaugurated by KingJuan Carlos Iin Bilbao, Spain
Zack de la Rocha announces he is leaving rock bandRage Against the Machine

After 8 years in exile,Benazir Bhuttoreturns to Pakistan. The same night, suicide attackers blow themselves up near Bhutto's convoy, killing over 100, including 20 police officers. Bhutto escapes uninjured.
QBTom Bradythrows five second quarter touchdowns against the Tennessee Titans, an NFL record for touchdown passes in one quarter
Mary-Kate OlsenandAshley Olsenare honoured for their work with fashion label The Row at the WSJ Magazine's Innovator Of The Year Awards in New York City
Rashid Khanmakes his One Day International (ODI) debut for Afghanistan against Zimbabwe
19th Chinese Communist Party congress opens in Beijing with PresidentXi Jinpingdelivering 3hr 23min speech heralding “new era”

The Johns Hopkins Center for Health Security, the World Economic Forum, and the Bill andMelinda GatesFoundation co-host "Event 201", a table-top panel exercise on dealing with a fictional high-level pandemic exercise in New York City
Marvel film "Eternals" directed byChloé Zhao, starring an ensemble cast including Gemma Chan,Richard MaddenandAngelina Joliepremieres in Los Angeles
The Girl Scouts of the USA announces receipt of donations totaling $84.5 million from American philanthropistMacKenzie Scott
Climate activistGreta Thunbergarrested at a demonstration outside an oil conference in London
US PresidentJoe Bidenmakes brief visit to Tel Aviv, Israel in attempt to ease tensions, pledging support for Israel but also urging caution
US takes formal possession of Alaska from Russia, after paying $7.2 million in the Alaska Purchase
The Treaty of Lausanne ends the Italo-Turkish War; Italy annexes Libya
US Olympic Committee suspends Tommie Smith andJohn Carlosfor giving the Black Power salute to protest racism and injustice against African-Americans during Olympic medal ceremony
